quote_of_day.text:
>>2089
It feels different when day gets wasted because of someone else, there's anger.
If I waste my day, there's just shame.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A high pressure system of racist and casteist remarks dominated the board, with a front of aggressive boasts and slurs sweeping across the Discourse section. This brought scattered showers of abusive language, with isolated thunderstorms of extreme hate speech.

A low-pressure trough of misogyny and homophobia moved into the Notices section, generating a drizzle of demeaning comments about Indian physiques and imageboard culture. The atmosphere remained oppressive, with a thick fog of sexism and transphobia reducing visibility.

In the Leading section, a cold snap of scientific discussion touched on the genetics of biracial children, but was quickly overrun by a downpour of trolling and insults.

Overall, the board remained overcast, with little chance of clear skies or constructive conversation.

articles.title: Anon Discusses Genetics of Biracial Children
articles.body:
Anon started a discussion about biracial children, mentioning "Gobarjeeta kumaris" and "GOBBARIDA!".

Another anon responded, citing a study that found 34 percent of biracial individuals in a national survey had been diagnosed with a psychological disorder, such as anxiety, depression, or substance abuse.

They also provided links to a UC Davis article on biracial Asian Americans and mental health.

A respondent argued that a child's unique genotype does not make them biologically incompatible, and that organ matching is based on HLA and blood type, not haplogroups.

They also pointed out that a higher rate of anxiety or depression in a group does not prove genetic damage.

Anon countered that even GPT admits the haplogroup and haplotype of a biracial baby is "different", and that organ donations from parents often do not work.

Another anon noted that hybrid vigor is a studied phenomenon, usually applied to farm animals, and does not necessarily mean that race mixing is healthier.

The conversation turned to the topic of hybrid vigor, with one anon stating that it is not a lottery thing and usually results in outbreeding depression.

Another respondent pointed out that interracial pregnancies have higher rates of miscarriages, stillbirths, and low birth weight because the mother's immune system attacks the fetus.

Anon also discussed the spiritual implications of biracial children, with one respondent stating that losing the haplogroup connection is a form of "spiritual severance".

The discussion also involved personal experiences and insults, with one anon asking about the smell of "AZN pussy" and another respondent making a comment about someone's mother.

Throughout the conversation, anon exchanged various views on genetics, mental health, and the implications of biracial children.

articles.title: Caste Boasts and Slurs
articles.body:
Anon posted that low-caste people are 'subhumans' and that upper castes are 'civilized and superior.' They also claimed that the Chinese have a better understanding of India's caste dynamics than white people.

Another anon responded by saying that low-caste people are viewed as 'loyal, cheap, and easily disposable servants' by the Chinese, while upper castes are seen as trying to assert their rights and freedoms.

This anon also mentioned that upper castes would be a problem for the Chinese.

A third anon claimed to be from a high caste in India and stated that Chinese people are lower than the lowest Dalit. They also used derogatory language to refer to Chinese people.

One respondent told the original poster that they wouldn't achieve anything by boasting about their caste and should instead focus on their studies.

Another anon expressed willingness to side with upper castes or the Chinese if it meant killing low-caste people.

The thread also included several personal attacks and insults, with one anon calling another a 'lowborn' and using derogatory language. Another anon threatened the original poster, saying 'death to OP.'

articles.title: Anon Seeks Safe Methods for Fucking Roadside Hijdas
articles.body:
Anon asked if anyone had experience fucking roadside hijdas and how to avoid getting a virus from them. The original poster sought safe methods for engaging in such activities.

Another anon warned about the risks, sharing a story from a friend who claimed someone got AIDS after a similar encounter.

This person allegedly agreed to pay for sex but was then fucked by the hijda as well, without it being part of the deal.

One respondent advised using condoms and not kissing, suggesting it was possible to find hijdas in shady areas of "tier 1" cities. They recommended not approaching alone and having a friend wait nearby.

Some anons expressed concern about the risks, with one stating they didn't want to get an STD.

Another simply advised against it, saying "don't do it, anon." A graphic scenario was shared about a friend of a friend who allegedly got roofied and date-raped by a MtF.

One poster drew a comparison, stating there's no difference between a "passing tranny" and an average person, while another shared a positive experience of meeting hijdas through Grindr, describing them as "very cool." The discussion included hostile reactions, with one anon questioning how anyone could engage in such activities without wanting to throw up.

Others were more direct in their warnings, with one saying the original poster would end up like someone else who allegedly got AIDS.

articles.title: Anon comments on Indian physiques
articles.body:
Anon started a thread saying 'Jhant bros..'.

Anon replied to an unseen post saying a comment was 'Funny but lets not forget average raju who you come across online descends from local bhillage zamindars who had nothing to do with rajput elite or royalty that had dealings with mughals.' Another anon asked, 'Why farming jeets from RJ, HR, and West UP have similar phenotype regardless of caste?

Most of them are indistinguishable.' Anon also made comments about an image, including 'those thighs are bigger than his skull', and 'Jhant brother and sister btw'.

One anon wrote 'You will smell like gobar' in response to another unseen post.

articles.title: Anon wonders about imageboard culture and women
articles.body: Anon asked why imageboard culture scares away women. Another anon replied that it's probably because imageboards are primarily made up of incels who don't know how to talk to women. One anon suggested that women should be scared to share anything online, calling it 'unfeminine.' Another claimed that imageboards attract contrarian viewpoints that women lack. An anon also mentioned that rape is a specific concern for women.
